bench: report at scale (Spec L, docs/specs/report-at-scale.md — runnable via bench/experiments/report-scale.mjs)

expandAll.note: finding #35: settledMsPerFrame ≤ 50 is the primary sim-cheap-per-frame target; maxSingleStepMs ≤ 250 is the no-single-frame-freezes floor the chunker guarantees. Both must hold for expandAllOk.

verdict: MEASURED, finding #35 landed. The graph view draws the domain overview first (built-in level-of-detail), and 'Expand all symbols' now seeds symbols on a golden-spiral hatch, adds a far-field monopole so repulsion has a long-range term (no more ±185k explosion), and runs the anneal in interruptible slices (gStepChunk) so no single task freezes a frame. At the 16.8k loaded corpus the worst single uninterruptible task drops from ~774ms (one whole gStep) to ~93ms — inside the 250ms no-freeze floor — but the far field is O(n·cells) and the settled per-step cost is ~270ms, above the 50ms/frame primary target. Per finding #35's fallback (reaching ≤50ms at this scale needs a hierarchical Barnes-Hut tree, out of the frozen scope): this ships at its best measured settle with maxSingleStepMs ≤ 250 as the honest FLOOR, not a pass — expandAllOk is true only where settledMsPerFrame ≤ 50 (holds through mid-thousands of symbols; the 16.8k row is the documented floor). The old 'GREEN at 16k, no fix needed' claim is retired.

rows.label: loaded corpus 16.8k
rows.expandAllMeasuredBy: node sim lab (bench/experiments/report-sim-lab.mjs, --domains 20 --per-domain 840 --sp 38 = 16800 synthetic nodes / 91883 edges, production 8ms slice budget) — every field in this expandAll block is the lab's, so nodes/edges are the lab's synthetic 16800/91883, NOT the report's real 17604/104143 (see `graph` above). The browser harness's staged-settle poll does not converge at 16.8k in-CI, so the sim numbers are the node micro-bench (spec-sanctioned primary evidence); the sliced gStepChunk path is bitwise-identical node↔browser (pinned in tests/report-sim.test.mjs), and the fixture row above validates the full browser path + the expandAll/drawOnce schema end-to-end with zero pageErrors. Reviewer re-ran the lab: settledMsPerFrame 274-302 (269 is honest, within box variance), maxSingleTaskMs 56-184 across 4 runs — all ≤250, the no-freeze floor holds robustly.

verdict.expandAllFallback: maxSingleStepMs 93.06 ≤ 250 (no-freeze floor MET; the chunker guarantees it); settledMsPerFrame 269.45 > 50 is the documented FLOOR (far field is O(n·cells) — ≤50ms needs a hierarchical tree). Before #35 the same click was one ~774ms uninterruptible task with a ±185k-px explosion.
verdict.greenNote: primary sim target not met at 16.8k by design of the frozen scope; shipped as the honest fallback floor, not a pass.
